_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.shape: The yield curve represents Treasury yields across maturities from 1 month to 30 years, typically sloping upward to reflect higher yields for longer maturities. This normal slope indicates expectations of economic growth and inflation. Currently, the 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.62% for a June 2026 maturity. The curve has re-steepened to a positive 10Y-2Y spread of 0.4 points, contrasting with the deepest inversion of 0.0 points seen during 2022-2024 when shorter maturities yielded more than longer ones.
notes.signal: The 10-year minus 2-year (2s10s) spread is a commonly watched recession indicator; an inverted spread (negative) has historically preceded US recessions by 6-18 months. From 2022 to 2024, the spread was inverted, signaling elevated recession risk. It has since un-inverted and steepened above zero to 0.4 points, which can indicate reduced immediate recession risk but may also occur shortly before a recession begins. The NY Fed recession probability currently stands at 31%, reflecting moderate concern but not consensus on near-term contraction.
notes.now: The yield curve's current modestly upward slope and positive 2s10s spread suggest a less immediate signal of economic downturn compared to the prior inversion period. However, the NY Fed's 31% recession probability indicates some ongoing concerns. Overall, the curve signals caution without definitively forecasting timing or certainty of a recession.
